# CLAUDE.md Generation

## Phase 1: Draft Synthesis

Use ultrathink. Synthesize a CLAUDE.md draft from:
- Discovery results (languages, frameworks, project shape)
- style.toml (conventions, formatters, linters, pre-commit)
- dominion.toml (project identity, direction, constraints)
- Wizard answers (user preferences, do's/don'ts, git workflow)
- Registry data (tool routing, MCP usage notes)

## Target: 150-300 lines, terse, directive.

### Section 1: Project (10-15 lines)
```markdown
<!-- Generated by Dominion — human-owned after init. Edit freely. -->
# Project Instructions

## Project
{name}: {vision}
Tech stack: {languages + frameworks}
{constraints if any}
```

### Section 2: Mandatory Rules (10-20 lines)
```markdown
## Mandatory Rules
{from dominion.toml [project.constraints.hard_rules]}
{from agent governance hard_stops — deduplicated}
- Do not make architectural decisions — stop and report
- Follow the documentation fallback chain — never source-dive
```

### Section 3: Language Conventions (30-60 lines)
One subsection per detected full/standard-level language:
```markdown
## {Language} Conventions
- Formatter: {from style.toml}
- Linter: {from style.toml}
- Error handling: {from style.toml}
- {each convention from style.toml, one line each}
- Pre-commit: {commands}
```

### Section 4: Tool Routing (15-25 lines)
```markdown
## Tool Routing
### Documentation Lookup Chain
{numbered list from dominion.toml [documentation.sources]}
TERMINAL: Stop and ask. Never read library source code.

### MCP Tools
- serena: code navigation, symbol analysis
- context7: API documentation lookup
- {other installed MCPs with one-line purpose}

### CLI
- `dominion-cli` — agent CLI for state, plans, signals
```

### Section 5: Git Conventions (15-20 lines)
```markdown
## Git Conventions
- Branch naming: {from wizard or detected}
- Commit format: {conventional commits / ticket-prefix / free-form}
- Merge strategy: {squash / rebase / merge}
- {any pet peeves from wizard}
```

### Section 6: Quality Gates (10-15 lines)
```markdown
## Quality Gates
Before committing, run:
{pre_commit commands from style.toml, per language}
```

### Section 7: Decision Framework (10-15 lines)
```markdown
## Decision Framework
- **Decide autonomously:** formatting, variable naming, test structure
- **Flag and continue:** minor deviations from plan, non-blocking issues
- **STOP and ask:** architectural decisions, new dependencies, wire format changes, security concerns
```

### Section 8: Dominion Integration (10-15 lines)
```markdown
## Dominion
- Agent roster: see AGENTS.md
- Agent configs: .dominion/agents/*.toml
- CLI: dominion-cli (run `dominion-cli --help`)
- Current state: `dominion-cli state resume`
- Documentation chain: {first → second → ... → STOP AND ASK}
```

## Phase 2: Section-by-Section Walkthrough

Present each section to the user one at a time:
```
Section N/8: {title}
  {section content}

  [approve / edit / skip]
```

- **approve** — keep as-is, move to next section
- **edit** — user provides modifications, apply them, re-present for confirmation
- **skip** — remove section from CLAUDE.md

After all sections reviewed, write the final CLAUDE.md to project root.
